goal:
Orchestrate goal — mcp-for-research (scholarly-research-mcp) — deep multi-day hardening and expansion

You are improving a production-quality TypeScript stdio MCP server for scholarly research. Entry: src/index.ts. Stack: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk, Zod, adapters under src/adapters/, preferences under src/preferences/. Published npm name: scholarly-research-mcp. License MIT. Repo is real traffic (GitHub stars); treat UX, honesty, and reliability as first-class.

North star: A researcher or coding agent should trust this MCP: schemas match behavior, errors are actionable, citations and identifiers are correct, docs match code, and npx -y scholarly-research-mcp always works. No secrets in git; env-only keys; .env.example stays accurate.

Non-negotiables: Never commit API keys. README "one-click" only where the client truly handoffs to install. Prefer multiple focused PRs over one unreviewable blob. Every mergeable unit: npm run build + npm test green (and any scripts touched by that unit). Update CHANGELOG.md for user-visible or schema changes.

Phase A — Truth, contracts, and schema hygiene (high priority)
Audit every tool (research_search, paper_analysis, citation_manager, research_preferences, web_research) against implementation. Produce an internal matrix: promised vs delivered. Then either implement or narrow Zod schemas and tool descriptions so models and users are never misled.

JSTOR: Today it appears in enums/prefs but is stripped in search. Choose: remove from public schema until a real adapter exists, or implement a minimal JSTOR path (only if legally/API-clear—if not, document "not available" in one consistent place, not scattered strings).

web_research: Actions include scrape/search vs extract/map/crawl. Either implement missing actions against the existing Firecrawl client wrapper, or remove undocumented/stub actions from the schema and README. Same for any Firecrawl params that are ignored.

paper_analysis: Replace "not implemented" placeholders with real behavior where data exists: PMC full-text excerpts, structured sections, optional keyword search within text, sensible truncation and token budgets. If LLM-based extraction is out of scope, do not fake quotes/statistics/findings—scope the tool honestly.

citation_manager: Implement real output for apa, mla, bibtex, ris, endnote (use a small well-tested formatter layer or vetted library—watch bundle size and license). If any format is unsupported, remove from enum. Fix the current "format label but one generic string" behavior. related: implement PubMed ELink (and ArXiv where feasible) or remove until real.

research_search: Ensure sortBy, date filters, sources, and preference overrides behave consistently in PreferenceAwareUnifiedSearchAdapter and documented. Surface per-source partial failures (e.g. Scholar blocked) instead of silent omission where possible.

research_preferences: Align cache and deduplication flags with actual behavior—implement bounded disk or memory cache with TTL from prefs, or remove/disable misleading toggles until implemented.

Phase B — Source adapters: robustness, performance, correctness
PubMed (pubmed.ts): Rate limiting, retries with backoff, clear messages for 429/API errors. Consolidate identifier resolution (PMID, PMCID, DOI). Ensure full-text path fails gracefully when PMC unavailable. Add or extend unit/integration tests with mocked HTTP where appropriate.

ArXiv (arxiv.ts): Parity for search and getPaperById; verify ID normalization matches index.ts helpers; tests for edge IDs and version suffixes.

Google Scholar: Document operational requirements (Chrome path, headless, Puppeteer version). Add timeouts, single-flight or concurrency limits, and user-visible errors when scraping fails or is blocked. Prefer Firecrawl when FIRECRAWL_API_KEY present and preferFirecrawl is true; document tradeoffs (ToS, reliability).

Firecrawl client (firecrawl-api-client.ts, google-scholar-firecrawl.ts): Typed errors, request timeouts, consistent response shaping. Tests with mocks.

Unified search: Deduplication quality (DOI, title+fuzzy, ArXiv id). Optional: merge duplicate hits across sources with a clear "same paper" presentation.

Phase C — MCP product quality (beyond "it runs")
Structured outputs: Where the SDK allows, return JSON (or application/json content parts) alongside human-readable markdown for at least research_search and citation_manager, so host apps can parse reliably.

Tool descriptions: Rewrite for agent consumption: required params, defaults, limits, when to use which tool, identifier formats, and known failure modes (Scholar captcha, missing Firecrawl, etc.).

Resources / prompts (optional): Add MCP resources only if high value (e.g. "identifier cheat sheet", "source coverage matrix"). Avoid bloat.

Versioning: Single source of truth for server version (read from package.json at build or export VERSION); align McpServer version string; semver discipline.

Phase D — Documentation and contributor experience
Rewrite stale docs: docs/ARCHITECTURE.md and any files still describing 24 tools or wrong file tree (src/models/ etc.). Align OVERVIEW.md, docs/API_REFERENCE.md, docs/PROJECT_STRUCTURE.md, README.md structure section.

Developer guide: docs/DEVELOPMENT.md — clone, npm ci, npm run build, npm test, how to run server locally, how to attach MCP in Cursor/VS Code, env vars table.

Troubleshooting: Common failures (npm exec, Puppeteer, Firecrawl, PubMed key), with copy-paste fixes.

CI (if missing or weak): GitHub Actions: npm ci, build, test on PR; optional lint/format if you add eslint/prettier with minimal config.

Phase E — Packaging, distribution, and smoke verification
package.json: Verify bin, main, files, engines; prepublishOnly runs build + pack validation. Extend scripts/validate-pack.js if needed so broken publishes are impossible.

Smoke test: Script or CI job that runs node dist/index.js with a minimal MCP handshake or tool list invocation (whatever is lightweight) so regressions in startup are caught.

Release checklist: Document bump + tag flow; ensure README note about stale npm latest stays accurate or fix the root cause permanently.

Phase F — Security, privacy, and policy
Dependency audit: npm audit; bump safe minors; document any accepted risk.

Logging: Ensure no query text or paper content is written to stdout in ways that break MCP stdio; use stderr for diagnostics consistently.

Scholar / scraping: README disclaimer: respect site terms, rate limits, institutional policies; prefer official APIs where available.

Phase G — Stretch (only after A–F stable)
Semantic Scholar or OpenAlex as additional metadata source (citations, OA links)—new adapter behind feature flag or env.

BibTeX import tool or mode for building a reading list from a .bib snippet.

Internationalization of date parsing (startDate/endDate formats).

Performance profiling on unified search (parallelize source queries with Promise.allSettled, cap concurrency).

Process expectations for orchestrate: Use verifiers on each major theme (schema honesty, PubMed, Scholar/Firecrawl, citations, docs, packaging). Prefer fewer, broader workers per planner.md unless tasks are truly independent. Merges are explicit tasks. Keep AGENTS.md workspace preferences respected.

Starting point: Prefer working from the repo's current default branch and any open agent setup branch (e.g. cursor/setup-agents-md-5a44) if that is where active work lives; rebase or merge from main as appropriate.

Definition of done (release bar): No stub parameters exposed; docs match code; tests + CI green; CHANGELOG reflects user-facing changes; npm pack validation passes; a new maintainer can onboard from README + docs/DEVELOPMENT.md in under 30 minutes.

That block is sized so a root planner can keep spawning workers and verifiers across themes A–G for a very long run without running out of scoped work. If you want even more load, add "write an e2e harness that drives all five tools against recorded HTTP fixtures" as its own verified epic.

tasks.name: phase-a-truth-contracts
tasks.scopedGoal: Phase A only (schema honesty): audit research_search, paper_analysis, citation_manager, research_preferences, web_research vs code; align Zod + tool descriptions with behavior; JSTOR / Firecrawl / prefs per root goal. Small PR(s), npm run build && npm test green, CHANGELOG for user-visible changes.
tasks.verify: build+test green; schemas and descriptions match implementation for touched tools.

tasks.name: verify-phase-a
tasks.scopedGoal: Verifier: confirm Phase A outcomes described in worker handoff; note gaps or approve.
